name: Appalachia Mountain Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the broken pieces of chocolate bars and marshmallow cream.
2. Grease a 9x13 inch cake pan with butter or margarine.
3. In a deep saucepan, mix together sugar, salt, evaporated milk, and butter over medium heat. Stir constantly until the mixture reaches a full boil.
4. Boil for exactly 13 minutes. Remove from the stove and stir in the chocolate pieces, marshmallow fluff, and melted chocolate.
5. Beat until smooth. Allow to cool completely in the refrigerator for about 2-3 hours before cutting into pieces.
6. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator to keep moist and creamy. This recipe makes a large quantity of fudge that can also be frozen without turning white. Additional nuts such as walnuts or pecans may be added.
